oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Cultural competence in the veterinary team and farm: Why should we care?
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Roman-Muniz, Noa
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: cultural competence
cultural humility
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The concept of cultural competence, well-described in human health care settings, has yet to be widely adopted in veterinary settings. Instead of mastering a checklist of cultural facts, ef­forts to improve cross-cultural relationships should include increasing self-awareness and a commitment to respectful partnerships and getting to know the individual. The ability to interact effectively with culturally diverse animal caretakers should strengthen relationships with clients, enhance animal health and well-being and improve the impact and profitability of the veterinary practice.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 111
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
